goal: Generalize the BP-146 one-shot permit path onto the BP-149 generic POST /probe/sandbox-write endpoint by reusing the existing smartPush writePermit path. The endpoint must require permitId for write operations, compute create/update coordinates from the actual sandbox file state, and block delete/undo until a dedicated permit type exists.
scope_out: Do not add a new permit store or duplicate maya-core permit logic.
Do not change smartPush, corridor, contentHash, GitHub adapter, server routing, package files, or deployment settings.
Do not perform a live GitHub write.
Do not enable or set runtime environment variables.
Do not implement delete/undo permits in this block; block delete until a separate permit type is contracted.
claims: POST /probe/sandbox-write requires permitId for write operations before GitHub write is attempted.
For write operations, the endpoint inspects sandbox file state, derives create/update and baseSha, then calls smartPush with targetRepo G-Dislioglu/bluepilot-sandbox and writePermit.
The existing smartPush permit path remains the single corridor consume point; no duplicate permit consume is built.
Delete operations are blocked before GitHub mutation because no dedicated delete/undo permit type exists yet.
Existing env gate and path/content validation remain fail-closed.
assumptions: BPK-001 is committed and pushed on bpk-001-doc-drift-hygiene.
BP-145 already implemented smartPush writePermit and corridor consume semantics.
BP-149 created the generic sandbox-write endpoint but did not bind it to permitId.
Delete/undo is safety-sensitive and needs a later explicit permit contract.
dod: builder/src/sandboxWrite.ts routes write operations through smartPush(writePermit) after read-only file-state inspection.
builder/src/sandboxWrite.ts rejects missing or malformed permitId before GitHub write.
builder/src/sandboxWrite.ts blocks delete operations before GitHub delete.
builder/tests/sandboxWrite.test.ts proves create/update permit wiring, missing permit block, corridor/write block propagation, and delete block.
review-packets/BPK-002.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
npm test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A maya-core code change becomes necessary.
A new permit store or duplicate consume implementation becomes necessary.
Package or dependency changes become necessary.
A live GitHub write or environment change becomes necessary.
Delete/undo cannot be safely blocked without broader product decision.
prior_task_findings: BPK-001 restored BP-149 anchor truth. Local code review found the generic sandbox-write endpoint already exists, while the existing permit consume path lives in smartPush(writePermit). BPK-002 should connect those existing pieces, not build a second permit mechanism.
